MINI MINUTES:

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALEXIS DAVIDSON, Esq. of The Michael Law Firm (Tel: 415-447-2833) for plaintiff;
Atty. LOWELL B. RITTER, Esq. of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP (Tel: 415-434-9100) for defendant Lyft, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Lyft, Inc.'s "motion to strike plaintiff's claim for punitive damages" is granted with leave to amend. The first amended complaint pleads no action by a Lyft officer, director or managing agent.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-23-2022 Continued To Jan-25-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Oct-10-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALEXIS L. DAVIDSON, Esq. of The Michael Law Firm (Tel: 415-447-2833) for plaintiff;
Atty. LOWELL B. RITTER, Esq. of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP (Tel: 415-434-9100) for defendant Lyft, Inc.;
Atty. MARISA L. D'AMICO, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming, LLP (Tel: 415-524-4303) for defendant Patrick Flynn dba Paddy Flynn's Irish Pub.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PATRICK FLYNN'S MOTION TO TRANSFER.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Patrick Flynn's motion to transfer this action to San Mateo County is denied.

The motion is made under Code of Civil Procedure 395, which provides that "the superior court in the county where the defendants or some of them reside at the commencement of the action is the proper court for the trial of the action." (Id.; Not. 2:3.) Where defendant Flynn himself resides is not clear; his counsel's declaration does not say. It is certain, however, that - as the first amended complaint (FAC) states - defendant Lyft, Inc. resides in San Francisco, which is "its principal place of business." (Id. at 3:3-5; Michael Dec. Ex. B.) Defendant Fasica Alemayehu is also pled to reside in San Francisco. (FAC 3:6-8.) Indeed, he was personally served summons at an address four blocks from the courthouse where trial would be held. (Michael Dec. Ex. B.) Thus, two of the three defendants reside in San Francisco, while none has been shown to reside in San Mateo County.

While he makes his motion under CCP 395 (Not. 2:3), Flynn invokes "forum non conveniens." His primary argument is witness convenience, but he names none. Moreover, judicial notice is taken of the facts that San Mateo County directly adjoins San Francisco and tens of thousands of people daily transit between the two counties.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
